methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
Sarla Aviation is a Series A transportation company founded by Rakesh Gaonkar and Adrian Schmidt. The target sector includes eight private peer companies across transportation, including REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger. No public comparables exist in the narrow peer set. The company has raised $10M to date.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and yields a $20M post-money valuation, using a 2.0x step-up multiplier from the $10M total raised proxy to account for typical Series A progression to subsequent stages. Peer-median analysis is not applicable; while five same-sector transportation peers exist, insufficient data points remain after filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables—the methodology requires a minimum of three datapoints to avoid misleading aggregation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $17M to $23M, with a median point of $20M. This range reflects a ±15% confidence interval around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the limited applicability of peer multiples due to the nascent, heterogeneous nature of the transportation tech sector and the absence of public market pricing signals. The valuation depends materially on execution against Series A milestones and investor appetite for frontier-stage transportation risk.
